title: Ocotea subalpina
taxonomy: Ocotea subalpina is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae. This family of plants is commonly known as laurel or lauraceae. The Lauraceae family is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The plants in this family are often aromatic and are used in various traditional and folk medicines. Members of the Lauraceae family include laurel trees, sassafras, and avocado trees.
uses: Ocotea subalpina is a plant species native to South America, and research suggests it has potential medicinal uses.
